mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within two weeks.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, invalidating the bearish signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or housing starts decline while consumption rises without affecting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below zero confirms supply-side contraction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 58% of months where industrial production contracts, breaking the causal link.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate earnings and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL against 20-day forward returns stays below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or equity returns remain positive during inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate below 4.0 percent confirms a healthy labor market, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, or payroll growth fails to translate into positive equity returns when unemployment is low.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent signals strong economic expansion, boosting corporate profit expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 2.5 percent, refuting the demand-driven bullish mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling federal funds rates indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting supply expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 2-year periods, or equity returns turn negative when industrial production rises during rate cuts.
